<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
# MD Reader

MD Reader is an Expo + React Native markdown reading environment built for immersive, offline-first mobile reading. The current repo bootstrap includes a local bookshelf, gesture-aware reader, autosaving editor, full-text search, theme system, and native HTML/PDF sharing.

## Included in This Repo

- Offline-first markdown library backed by Expo FileSystem and Expo SQLite
- Bookshelf-style library with grid/list toggle, continue reading, favorites, pins, pull-to-refresh, and local import
- Reader screen with center-tap chrome toggle, swipe-down dismiss gesture, progress tracking, resume position, and font scaling
- Markdown editor with live preview, autosave, and shortcut toolbar
- Search across file titles and content with recent queries and highlighted matches
- Theme system with light, dark, AMOLED, sepia, and system modes
- Native export and share support via HTML and PDF

## Stack

- Expo SDK 54
- React Native 0.81
- React Navigation native stack
- React Native Reanimated
- React Native Gesture Handler
- Expo FileSystem
- Expo SQLite
- Expo Sharing and Expo Print
- react-native-markdown-display

## Run

```bash
npm install
npm start
```

Useful scripts:

- `npm run android`
- `npm run ios`
- `npm run web`
- `npx tsc --noEmit`

## Notes

- The initial library is seeded with sample markdown files so the reader is usable immediately.
- Imported files are copied into the app sandbox and tracked in SQLite metadata.
- The project is initialized as a git repository and includes a VS Code task for starting the Expo dev server.
- Full system share-menu ingestion and advanced image pinch-to-zoom are not yet wired as native extensions; the current bootstrap uses document picker import and inline image rendering as the base implementation.
